steps.title: Prepare the magnet-coated beads
steps.instruction: Prepare the magnet-coated beads. For this protocol, the Dynabeads M-280 Tosylactivated beads are used

steps.title: Resuspend the Dynabeads in the vial by vortexing for 00:00:30
steps.instruction: Resuspend the Dynabeads in the vial by vortexing for 00:00:30

steps.title: Transfer 300 µL of the beads...
steps.instruction: Transfer 300 µL of the beads to a new, sterile 1.5 mL tube

steps.title: Add 1 mL of Buffer B...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of Buffer B to the tube and vortex for 00:00:30 A B C 1 Reagent Molecular weight (g/mol) Amount needed 2 NaH2PO4 × H2O 137.99 2.62 g 3 Na2HPO4 × 2 H2O 177.99 14.42 g 4 NaOH or HCl – pH 7.4 5 Distilled water (H2O) 18.015 Fill to 1 L Buffer B composition

steps.title: Place the tube in a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube in a magnet for 00:01:00 and discard the supernatant. Repeat go to step #1.3 once

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnet...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnet and resuspend the washed beads in 300 µL of Buffer B. Divide the washed beads into two tubes, containing 150 µL each

steps.title: Couple the antibodies covalently with Dynabeads...
steps.instruction: Couple the antibodies covalently with Dynabeads M-280 Tosylactivated using the recommended protocol from the manufacturer. For this protocol, the anti-TGF-β1 and anti-PD-L1 antibodies will be used. However, different antibody reagents can be subjected for coupling

steps.title: Place the tubes in a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tubes in a magnet for 00:01:00 and discard the supernatant

steps.title: For every 150 µL of Dynabeads...
steps.instruction: For every 150 µL of Dynabeads as the initial volume, resuspend in 100 µg/150 µL of antibodies (anti-TGF-β1 or anti-PD-L1) diluted using Buffer B. Mix by vortexing for 00:00:30

steps.title: Add 100 µL Buffer C and...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL Buffer C and mix by vortexing for 00:00:30 A B 1 Reagent Amount needed 2 [(NH4)2]SO4 39.64 g 3 Buffer B 100 mL 4 NaOH or HCl pH 7.4 Buffer C composition

steps.title: Incubate on a shaker at 37 °C for 16:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate on a shaker at 37 °C for 16:00:00

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnet for 00:02:00 and remove the supernatant

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnet...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnet and add 1 mL Buffer D; incubate at 37 °C for 01:00:00 on a shaker. A B 1 Reagent Amount needed 2 NaCl 0.88 g 3 BSA (Bovine Serum Albumin) 0.5% (w/v) 4 Buffer B 80 mL 5 NaOH or HCl pH 7.4 Buffer D composition

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnet for 00:02:00 and remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnet...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnet and add 1 mL Buffer E; vortex for 00:00:10

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnet for 00:02:00 and remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat steps go to step #2
steps.instruction: Repeat steps go to step #2.7 and go to step #2.8 once.

steps.title: For every 150 µL of Dynabeads,...
steps.instruction: For every 150 µL of Dynabeads, resuspend and dilute the beads in 200 µL of Buffer E. %s %s

steps.title: Following transfection, harvest HEK 293 cells after 48:00:00 of incubation
steps.instruction: Following transfection, harvest HEK 293 cells after 48:00:00 of incubation.

steps.title: For every 1 × 10⁶ cells,...
steps.instruction: For every 1 × 10⁶ cells, resuspend using 500 µL of native protein extraction buffer. A B 1 Reagent Amount needed 2 Tris 50 mM 3 EDTA 1 mM 4 SDS 0.001% (w/v) 5 Tween-20 0.01% (w/v) 6 NaCl 50 mM Composition of the native protein extraction buffer, pH 7.4

steps.title: Decant the culture medium and wash...
steps.instruction: Decant the culture medium and wash the cells twice with sterile PBS.

steps.title: Submerge the cells in PBS and...
steps.instruction: Submerge the cells in PBS and incubate at 25 °C for 00:01:00

steps.title: Tap the sides of the plate...
steps.instruction: Tap the sides of the plate gently to detach the cells and transfer the supernatant.

steps.title: Collect the cells by centrifugation at...
steps.instruction: Collect the cells by centrifugation at 500 x g, 25 °C, 5 minutes

steps.title: Lyse the cells by passing the...
steps.instruction: Lyse the cells by passing the solution through a 23-G needle syringe 15 times. This should be done gently and on ice.

steps.title: Clarify the lysate by centrifugation at...
steps.instruction: Clarify the lysate by centrifugation at 500 x g, 25 °C, 5 minutes . Transfer the supernatant to a new sterile tube, and store the pellet at -80 °C for future protein recovery.

steps.title: Quantify the protein content of the extract
steps.instruction: Quantify the protein content of the extract. Any protein quantification method available in the laboratory may be used.

steps.title: Add 10 µg of sample containing...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µg of sample containing the recombinant protein to 50 µL of the antibody-coupled beads. For this protocol, a bifunctional fusion protein that contains the TGF-B1 and PD-L1 domains are used. Prepare two separate reactions for each antibody-coupled Dynabeads (anti-TGF-β1 or anti-PD-L1)

steps.title: Incubate with tilting and rotation for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate with tilting and rotation for 00:30:00

steps.title: Place the tube on a magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on a magnet for 00:02:00 and pipette off the supernatant

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnet...
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnet and add 1 mL PBS; vortex for 00:00:10

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnet for 00:02:00 and remove the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat steps go to step #11...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps go to step #11 and go to step #12 twice

steps.title: Add 50 µL of 100 mM glycine-HCl ( pH 3
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of 100 mM glycine-HCl ( pH 3.0 ) and incubate for 00:01:00

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnet for 00:02:00 and collect the supernatant

steps.title: Neutralize the collected supernatant by adding...
steps.instruction: Neutralize the collected supernatant by adding 5 µL of 1 M Tris-HCl ( pH 8.8 ). At this point, the beads may also be washed with 50 µL of 1 M Tris-HCl for reuse in future isolations.

steps.title: Quantify the concentration of the isolated...
steps.instruction: Quantify the concentration of the isolated protein and analyze the purity using SDS-PAGE or other methods
